text: And Moses gave a possession to the children of Ruben according to their kindreds.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And their border was from Aroer, which is on the bank of the torrent Arnon, and in the midst of the valley of the same torrent: all the plain, that leadeth to Medaba,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Hesebon, and all their villages, which are in the plains. Dibon also, and Bamothbaal, and the town of Baalmaon,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jassa, and Cidimoth, and Mephaath,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Cariathaim, and Sabama, and Sarathasar in the mountain of the valley.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Bethphogor and Asedoth, Phasga and Bethiesimoth,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all the cities of the plain, and all the kingdoms of Sehon king of the Amorrhites, that reigned in Hesebon, whom Moses slew with the princes of Madian: Hevi, and Recem, and Sur and Hur, and Rebe, dukes of Sehon inhabitants of the land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Balaam also the son of Beor the soothsayer, the children of Israel slew with the sword among the rest that were slain.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the river Jordan was the border of the children of Ruben. This is the possession of the Rubenites, by their kindreds, of cities and villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses gave to the tribe of Gad and to his children by their kindreds a possession, of which this is the division.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The border of Jaser, and all the cities of Galaad, and half the land of the children of Ammon: as far as Aroer which is over against Rabba:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And from Hesebon unto Ramoth, Masphe and Betonim: and from Manaim unto the borders of Dabir.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the valley Betharan and Bethnemra, and Socoth, and Saphon the other part of the kingdom of Sehon king of Hesebon: the limit of this also is the Jordan, as far as the uttermost part of the sea of Cenereth beyond the Jordan on the east side,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the possession of the children of Gad by their families, their cities, and villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He gave also to the half tribe of Manasses and his children possession according to their kindreds,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The beginning whereof is this: from Manaim all Basan, and all the kingdoms of Og king of Basan, and all the villages of Jair, which are in Basan, threescore towns.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And half Galaad, and Astaroth, and Edrai, cities of the kingdom of Og in Basan: to the children of Machir, the son of Manasses, to one half of the children of Machir according to their kindreds.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This possession Moses divided in the plains of Moab, beyond the Jordan, over against Jericho on the east side,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But to the tribe of Levi he gave no possession: because the Lord the God of Israel himself is their possession, as he spoke to them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is what the children of Israel possessed in the land of Chanaan, which Eleazar the priest, and Josue the son of Nun, and the princes of the families by the tribes of Israel gave to them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Dividing all by lot, as the Lord had commanded by the hand of Moses, to the nine tribes, and the half tribe.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For to two tribes and a half Moses had given possession beyond the Jordan: besides the Levites, who received no land among their brethren:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But in their place succeeded the children of Joseph divided into two tribes, of Manasses and Ephraim: neither did the Levites receive other portion of land, but cities to dwell in, and their suburbs to feed their beasts and flocks.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As the Lord had commanded Moses so did the children of Israel, and they divided the land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then the children of Juda came to Josue in Galgal, and Caleb the son of Jephone the Cenezite spoke to him: Thou knowest what the Lord spoke to Moses the man of God concerning me and thee in Cadesbarne.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I was forty years old when Moses the servant of the Lord sent me from Cadesbarne, to view the land, and I brought him word again as to me seemed true,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But my brethren, that had gone up with me, discouraged the heart of the people: and I nevertheless followed the Lord my God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses swore in that day, saying: The land which thy foot hath trodden upon shall be thy possession, and thy children’s for ever, because thou hast followed the Lord my God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord therefore hath granted me life, as he promised until this present day, It is forty and five years since the Lord spoke this word to Moses, when Israel journeyed through the wilderness: this day I am eighty-five years old,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As strong as I was at that time when I was sent to view the land: the strength of that time continueth in me until this day, as well to fight as to march.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Give me therefore this mountain, which the Lord promised, in thy hearing also, wherein are the Enacims, and cities great and strong: if so be the Lord will be with me, and I shall be able to destroy them, as he promised me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Josue blessed him, and gave him Hebron in possession.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And from that time Hebron belonged to Caleb the son of Jephone the Cenezite, until this present day: because he followed the Lord the God of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The name of Hebron before was called Cariath-Arbe: Adam the greatest among the Enacims was laid there and the land rested from wars.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now the lot of the children of Juda by their kindreds was this: From the frontier of Edom, to the desert of Sin southward, and to the uttermost part of the south coast.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Its beginning was from the top of the most salt sea, and from the bay thereof, that looketh to the south.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it goeth out towards the ascent of the Scorpion, and passeth on to Sina: and ascendeth into Cadesbarne, and reacheth into Esron, going up to Addar, and compassing Carcaa.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And from thence passing along into Asemona, and reaching the torrent of Egypt: and the bounds thereof shall be the great sea, this shall be the limit of the south coast.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But on the east side the beginning shall be the most salt sea even to the end of the Jordan: and towards the north from the bay of the sea unto the same river Jordan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the border goeth up into Beth-Hagla, and passeth by the north into Beth-Araba: going up to the stone of Boen the son of Ruben.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And reaching as far as the borders of Debara from the valley of Achor, and so northward looking towards Galgal, which is opposite to the ascent of Adommin, on the south side of the torrent, and the border passeth the waters that are called the fountain of the sun: and the goings out thereof shall be at the fountain Rogel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it goeth up by the valley of the son of Ennom on the side of the Jebusite towards the south, the same is Jerusalem: and thence ascending to the top of the mountain, which is over against Geennom to the west in the end of the valley of Raphaim, northward.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it passeth on from the top of the mountain to the fountain of the water of Nephtoa: and reacheth to the towns of mount Ephron: and it bendeth towards Baala, which is Cariathiarim, that is to say, the city of the woods.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it compasseth from Baala westward unto mount Seir: and passeth by the side of mount Jarim to the north into Cheslon: and goeth down into Bethsames, and passeth into Thamna.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And reacheth northward to a part of Accaron at the side: and bendeth to Sechrona, and passeth mount Baala: and cometh into Jebneel, and is bounded westward with the great sea.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the borders round about of the children of Juda in their kindreds.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But to Caleb the son of Jephone he gave a portion in the midst of the children of Juda, as the Lord had commanded him: Cariath-Arbe the father of Enac, which is Hebron.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Caleb destroyed out of it the three sons of Enac, Sesai and Ahiman, and Tholmai of the race of Enac.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And going up from thence he came to the inhabitants of Dabir, which before was called Cariath-Sepher, that is to say, the city of letters.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Caleb said: He that shall smite Cariath-Sepher, and take it, I will give him Axa my daughter to wife.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Othoniel the son of Cenez, the younger brother of Caleb, took it: and he gave him Axa his daughter to wife.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as they were going together, she was moved by her husband to ask a field of her father, and she sighed as she sat on her ass. And Caleb said to her: What aileth thee?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But she answered: Give me a blessing: thou hast given me a southern and dry land, give me also a land that is watered. And Caleb gave her the upper and the nether watery ground.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the possession of the tribe of the children of Juda by their kindreds.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the cities from the uttermost parts of the children of Juda by the borders of Edom to the south, were Cabseel and Eder and Jagur,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Cina and Dimona and Adada,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Cades and Asor and Jethnam,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Ziph and Telem and Baloth,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: New Asor and Carioth, Hesron, which is Asor.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Amam, Sama and Molada,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Asergadda and Hassemon and Bethphelet,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Hasersual and Bersabee and Baziothia,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Baala and Jim and Esem,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Eltholad and Cesil and Harma,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Siceleg and Medemena and Sensenna,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lebaoth and Selim and Aen and Remmon: all the cities twenty-nine, and their villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But in the plains: Estaol and Sarea and Asena,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Zanoe and Engannim and Taphua and Enaim,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jerimoth and Adullam, Socho and Azeca,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Saraim and Adithaim and Gedera and Gederothaim: fourteen cities, and their villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Sanan and Hadassa and Magdalgad,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Delean and Masepha and Jecthel,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lachis and Bascath and Eglon,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Chebbon and Leheman and Cethlis,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Gideroth and Bethdagon and Naama and Maceda: sixteen cities, and their villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Labana and Ether and Asan,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jephtha and Esna and Nesib,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Ceila and Achzib and Maresa: nine cities, and their villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Accaron with the towns and villages thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From Accaron even to the sea: all places that lie towards Azotus and the villages thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Azotus with its towns and villages. Gaza with its towns and villages, even to the torrent of Egypt, and the great sea that is the border thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the mountain Samir and Jether and Socoth,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Danna and Cariath-senna, this is Dabir:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Anab and Istemo and Anim,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Gosen and Olon and Gilo: eleven cities and their villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Arab and Ruma and Esaan,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Janum and Beththaphua and Apheca,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Athmatha and Cariath-Arbe, this is Hebron and Sior: nine cities and their villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Maon and Carmel and Ziph and Jota,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jezrael and Jucadam and Zanoe,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Accain, Gabaa and Thamna: ten cities and their villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Halhul, and Bessur, and Gedor,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Mareth, and Bethanoth, and Eltecon: six cities and their villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cariathbaal, the same is Cariathiarim the city of woods, and Arebba: two cities and their villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the desert Betharaba, Meddin and Sachacha,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Nebsan, and the city of salt, and Engaddi: six cities and their villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the children of Juda could not destroy the Jebusite that dwelt in Jerusalem: and the Jebusite dwelt with the children of Juda in Jerusalem until this present day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the lot of the sons of Joseph fell from the Jordan over against Jericho and the waters thereof, on the east: the wilderness which goeth up from Jericho to the mountain of Bethel:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And goeth out from Bethel to Luza: and passeth the border of Archi, to Ataroth,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And goeth down westward, by the border of Jephleti, unto the borders of Beth-horon the nether, and to Gazer: and the countries of it are ended by the great sea:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Manasses and Ephraim the children of Joseph possessed it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the border of the children of Ephraim was according to their kindreds: and their possession towards the east was Ataroth-addar unto Beth-horon the upper.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the confines go out unto the sea: but Machmethath looketh to the north, and it goeth round the borders eastward into Thanath-selo: and passeth along on the east side to Janoe.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it goeth down from Janoe into Ataroth and Naaratha: and it cometh to Jericho, and goeth out to the Jordan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From Taphua it passeth on towards the sea into the valley of reeds, and the goings out thereof are at the most salt sea. This is the possession of the tribe of the children of Ephraim by their families.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there were cities with their villages separated for the children of Ephraim in the midst of the possession of the children of Manasses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of Ephraim slew not the Chanaanite, who dwelt in Gazer: and the Chanaanite dwelt in the midst of Ephraim until this day, paying tribute.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And this lot fell to the tribe of Manasses for he is the firstborn of Joseph to Machir the firstborn of Manasses the father of Galaad, who was a warlike man, and had for possession Galaad and Basan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And to the rest of the children of Manasses according to their families: to the children of Abiezer, and to the children of Helec, and to the children of Esriel, and to the children of Sechem, and to the children of Hepher, and to the children of Semida: these are the male children of Manasses the son of Joseph, by their kindreds.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But Salphaad the son of Hepher the son of Galaad the son of Machir the son of Manasses had no sons, but only daughters: whose names are these, Maala and Noa and Hegla and Melcha and Thersa.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they came in the presence of Eleazar the priest and of Josue the son of Nun, and of the princes, saying: The Lord commanded by the hand of Moses, that a possession should be given us in the midst of our brethren. And he gave them according to the commandment of the Lord a possession amongst the brethren of their father.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there fell ten portions to Manasses, beside the land of Galaad and Basan beyond the Jordan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the daughters of Manasses possessed inheritance in the midst of his sons. And the land of Galaad fell to the lot of the rest of the children of Manasses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the border of Manasses was from Aser, Machmethath which looketh towards Sichem: and it goeth out on the right hand by the inhabitants of the fountain of Taphua.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the lot of Manasses took in the land of Taphua, which is on the borders of Manasses, and belongs to the children of Ephraim.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the border goeth down to the valley of the reeds, to the south of the torrent of the cities of Ephraim, which are in the midst of the cities of Manasses: the border of Manasses is on the north side of the torrent, and the outgoings of it are at the sea:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So that the possession of Ephraim is on the south, and on the north that of Manasses, and the sea is the border of both, and they are joined together in the tribe of Aser on the north, and in the tribe of Issachar on the east.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the inheritance of Manasses in Issachar and in Aser, was Bethsan and its villages, and Jeblaam with its villages, and the inhabitants of Dor, with the towns thereof: the inhabitants also of Endor with the villages thereof: and in like manner the inhabitants of Thenac with the villages thereof: and the inhabitants of Mageddo with their villages, and the third part of the city of Nopheth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither could the children of Manasses overthrow these cities, but the Chanaanite began to dwell in his land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But after that the children of Israel were grown strong, they subdued the Chanaanites, and made them their tributaries, and they did not kill them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of Joseph spoke to Josue, and said: Why hast thou given me but one lot and one portion to possess, whereas I am of so great a multitude, and the Lord hath blessed me?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Josue said to them: If thou be a great people, go up into the woodland, and cut down room for thyself in the land of the Pherezite and the Raphaims: because the possession of mount Ephraim is too narrow for thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of Joseph answered him: We cannot go up to the mountains, for the Chanaanites that dwell in the low lands, wherein are situate Bethsan with its towns, and Jezrael in the midst of the valley, have chariots of iron.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Josue said to the house of Joseph, to Ephraim and Manasses: Thou art a great people, and of great strength, thou shalt not have one lot only:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But thou shalt pass to the mountain, and shalt cut down the wood, and make thyself room to dwell in: and mayst proceed farther, when thou hast destroyed the Chanaanites, who as thou sayest have iron chariots, and are very strong.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all the children of Israel assembled together in Silo, and there they set up the tabernacle of the testimony, and the land was subdued before them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But there remained seven tribes of the children of Israel, which as yet had not received their possessions.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Josue said to them: How long are you indolent and slack, and go not in to possess the land which the Lord the God of your fathers hath given you?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Choose of every tribe three men, that I may send them, and they may go and compass the land, and mark it out according to the number of each multitude: and bring back to me what they have marked out.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Divide to yourselves the land into seven parts: let Juda be in his bounds on the south side, and the house of Joseph on the north.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The land in the midst between these mark ye out into seven parts; and you shall come hither to me, that I may cast lots for you before the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the Levites have no part among you, but the priesthood of the Lord is their inheritance. And Gad and Ruben, and the half tribe of Manasses have already received their possessions beyond the Jordan eastward: which Moses the servant of the Lord gave them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the men were risen up, to go to mark out the land, Josue commanded them saying: Go round the land and mark it out, and return to me: that I may cast lots for you before the Lord in Silo.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So they went and surveying it divided it into seven parts, writing them down in a book. And they returned to Josue, to the camp in Silo.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he cast lots before the Lord in Silo, and divided the land to the children of Israel into seven parts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And first came up the lot of the children of Benjamin by their families, to possess the land between the children of Juda, and the children of Joseph.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And their border northward was from the Jordan: going along by the side of Jericho on the north side, and thence going up westward to the mountains, and reaching to the wilderness of Bethaven,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And passing along southward by Luza, the same is Bethel, and it goeth down into Ataroth-addar to the mountain, that is on the south of the nether Beth-horon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it bendeth thence going round towards the sea, south of the mountain that looketh towards Beth-horon to the south-west: and the outgoings thereof are into Cariathbaal, which is called also Cariathiarim, a city of the children of Juda This is their coast towards the sea, westward.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But on the south side the border goeth out from part of Cariathiarim towards the sea, and cometh to the fountain of the waters of Nephtoa.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it goeth down to that part of the mountain that looketh on the valley of the children of Ennom: and is over against the north quarter in the furthermost part of the valley of Raphaim, and it goeth down into Geennom (that is the valley of Ennom) by the side of the Jebusite to the south: and cometh to the fountain of Rogel,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Passing thence to the north, and going out to Ensemes, that is to say, the fountain of the sun:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it passeth along to the hills that are over against the ascent of Adommim: and it goeth down to Abenboen, that is, the stone of Boen the son of Ruben: and it passeth on the north side to the champaign countries; and goeth down into the plain,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it passeth by Bethhagla northward: and the outgoings thereof are towards the north of the most salt sea at the south end of the Jordan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which is the border of it on the east side. This is the possession of the children of Benjamin by their borders round about, and their families.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And their cities were, Jericho and Bethhagla and Vale-Casis,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Betharaba and Samaraim and Bethel,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Avim and Aphara and Ophera,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The town Emona and Ophni and Gabee: twelve cities, and their villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Gabam and Rama and Beroth,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Mesphe, and Caphara, and Amosa,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Recem, Jarephel, and Tharela,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Sela, Eleph and Jebus, which is Jerusalem, Gabaath and Cariath: fourteen cities, and their villages. This is the possession of the children of Benjamin by their families.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the second lot came forth for the children of Simeon by their kindreds: and their inheritance was

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the midst of the possession of the children of Juda: Bersabee and Sabee and Molada,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Hasersual, Bala and Asem,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Eltholad, Bethul and Harma,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Siceleg and Bethmarchaboth and Hasersusa,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Bethlebaoth and Sarohen: thirteen cities, and their villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Remmon and Athor and Asan: four cities, and their villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all the villages round about these cities to Baalath Beer Ramath to the south quarter. This is the inheritance of the children of Simeon according to their kindreds,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the possession and lot of the children of Juda: because it was too great, and therefore the children of Simeon had their possession in the midst of their inheritance.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the third lot fell to the children of Zabulon by their kindreds: and the border of their possession was unto Sarid.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it went up from the sea and from Merala, and came to Debbaseth: as far as the torrent, which is over against Jeconam.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it returneth from Sarid eastward to the borders of Ceseleththabor: and it goeth out to Dabereth and ascendeth towards Japhie.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it passeth along from thence to the east side of Gethhepher and Thacasin: and goeth out to Remmon, Amthar and Noa.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it turneth about to the north of Hanathon: and the outgoings thereof are the valley of Jephtahel,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Cateth and Naalol and Semeron and Jedala and Bethlehem: twelve cities and their villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the inheritance of the tribe of the children of Zabulon by their kindreds, the cities and their villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The fourth lot came out to Issachar by their kindreds.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his inheritance was Jezrael and Casaloth and Sunem,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Hapharaim and Seon and Anaharath,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Rabboth and Cesion, Abes,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Rameth and Engannim and Enhadda and Bethpheses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the border thereof cometh to Thabor and Sehesima and Bethsames: and the outgoings thereof shall be at the Jordan: sixteen cities, and their villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the possession of the sons of Issachar by their kindreds, the cities and their villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the fifth lot fell to the tribe of the children of Aser by their kindreds:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And their border was Halcath and Chali and Beten and Axaph,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Elmelech and Amaad and Messal: and it reacheth to Carmel by the sea and Sihor and Labanath,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it returneth towards the east to Bethdagon: and passeth along to Zabulon and to the valley of Jephthael towards the north to Bethemec and Nehiel. And it goeth out to the left side of Cabul,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And to Abaran and Rohob and Hamon and Cana, as far as the great Sidon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it returneth to Horma to the strong city of Tyre, and to Hosa: and the outgoings thereof shall be at the sea from the portion of Achziba:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Amma and Aphec and Rohob: twenty-two cities, and their villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the possession of the children of Aser by their kindreds, and the cities and their villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sixth lot came out to the sons of Nephtali by their families:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the border began from Heleph and Elon to Saananim, and Adami, which is Neceb, and Jebnael even to Lecum: and their outgoings unto the Jordan:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the border returneth westward to Azanotthabor, and goeth out from thence to Hucuca, and passeth along to Zabulon southward, and to Aser westward, and to Juda upon the Jordan towards the rising of the sun.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the strong cities are Assedim, Ser, and Emath, and Reccath and Cenereth,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Edema and Arama, Asor,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Cedes and Edri, Enhasor,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jeron and Magdalel, Horem, and Bethanath and Bethsames: nineteen cities, and their villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the possession of the tribe of the children of Nephtali by their kindreds, the cities and their villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The seventh lot came out to the tribe of the children of Dan by their families:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the border of their possession was Saraa and Esthaol, and Hirsemes, that is, the city of the sun,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Selebin and Aialon and Jethela,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Elon and Themna and Acron,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Elthece, Gebbethon and Balaath,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Juda and Bane and Barach and Gethremmon:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Mejarcon and Arecon, with the border that looketh towards Joppe,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And is terminated there. And the children of Dan went up and fought against Lesem, and took it: and they put it to the sword, and possessed it, and dwelt in it, calling the name of it Lesem Dan, by the name of Dan their father.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the possession of the tribe of the sons of Dan, by their kindreds, the cities and their villages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he had made an end of dividing the land by lot to each one by their tribes, the children of Israel gave a possession to Josue the son of Nun in the midst of them,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: According to the commandment of the Lord, the city which he asked for, Thamnath Saraa, in mount Ephraim: and he built up the city, and dwelt in it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the possessions which Eleazar the priest, and Josue the son of Nun, and the princes of the families, and of the tribes of the children of Israel, distributed by lot in Silo, before the Lord at the door of the tabernacle of the testimony, and they divided the land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Josue, saying: Speak to the children of Israel and say to them:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Appoint cities of refuge, of which I spoke to you by the hand of Moses:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That whosoever shall kill a person unawares may flee to them, and may escape the wrath of the kinsman, who is the avenger of blood.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he shall flee to one of these cities: he shall stand before the gate of the city, and shall speak to the ancients of that city, such things as prove him innocent: and so shall they receive him, and give him a place to dwell in.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the avenger of blood shall pursue him, they shall not deliver him into his hands, because he slew his neighbour unawares, and is not proved to have been his enemy two or three days before,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall dwell in that city, till he stand before judgment to give an account of his fact, and till the death of the high priest, who shall be at that time: then shall the manslayer return, and go into his own city and house from whence he fled.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they appointed Cedes in Galilee of mount Nephtali, and Sichem in mount Ephraim, and Cariath-Arbe, the same is Hebron in the mountain of Juda.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And beyond the Jordan to the east of Jericho, they appointed Bosor, which is upon the plain of the wilderness of the tribe of Ruben, and Ramoth in Galaad of the tribe of Gad, and Gaulon in Basan of the tribe of Manasses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These cities were appointed for all the children of Israel, and for the strangers, that dwelt among them, that whosoever had killed a person unawares might flee to them, and not die by the hand of the kinsman, coveting to revenge the blood that was shed, until he should stand before the people to lay open his cause.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then the princes of the families of Levi came to Eleazar the priest, and to Josue the son of Nun, and to the princes of the kindreds of all the tribes of the children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they spoke to them in Silo in the land of Chanaan, and said: The Lord commanded by the hand of Moses, that cities should be given us to dwell in, and their suburbs to feed our cattle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of Israel gave out of their possessions according to the commandment of the Lord, cities and their suburbs.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the lot came out for the family of Caath of the children of Aaron the priest out of the tribes of Juda, and of Simeon, and of Benjamin, thirteen cities.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And to the rest of the children of Caath, that is, to the Levites, who remained, out of the tribes of Ephraim, and of Dan, and the half tribe of Manasses, ten cities.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the lot came out to the children of Gerson, that they should take of the tribes of Issachar and of Aser and of Nephtali, and of the half tribe of Manasses in Basan, thirteen cities.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And to the sons of Merari by their kindreds, of the tribes of Ruben and of Gad and of Zabulon, twelve cities.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of Israel gave to the Levites the cities and their suburbs, as the Lord commanded by the hand of Moses, giving to every one by lot.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribes of the children of Juda and of Simeon Josue gave cities: whose names are these,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To the sons of Aaron, of the families of Caath of the race of Levi (for the first lot came out for them)

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The city of Arbe the father of Enac, which is called Hebron, in the mountain of Juda, and the suburbs thereof round about.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the fields and the villages thereof he had given to Caleb the son of Jephone for his possession.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He gave therefore to the children of Aaron the priest, Hebron a city of refuge, and the suburbs thereof, and Lebna with the suburbs thereof,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jether and Estemo,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Holon, and Dabir,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Ain, and Jeta, and Bethsames, with their suburbs: nine cities out of the two tribes, as hath been said.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And out of the tribe of the children of Benjamin, Gabaon, and Gabae,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Anathoth and Almon, with, their suburbs: four cities.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the cities together of the children of Aaron the priest, were thirteen, with their suburbs,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And to the rest of the families of the children of Caath of the race of Levi was given this possession.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of Ephraim, Sichem one of the cities of refuge, with the suburbs thereof in mount Ephraim, and Gazer,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Cibsaim, and Beth-horon, with their suburbs, four cities.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And of he tribe of Dan, Eltheco and Gabathon,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Aialon and Gethremmon, with their suburbs, four cities.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And of the half tribe of Manasses, Thanac and Gethremmon, with their suburbs, two cities.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the cities were ten, with their suburbs, which were given to the children of Caath, of the inferior degree.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To the children of Gerson also of the race of Levi out of the half tribe of Manasses, Gaulon in Basan, one of the cities of refuge, and Bosra, with their suburbs, two cities.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And of the tribe of Issachar, Cesion, and Dabereth,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jaramoth, and Engannim, with their suburbs, four cities.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And of the tribe of Aser, Masal and Abdon,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Helcath, and Rohob, with their suburbs, four cities.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe also of Nephtali, Cedes in Galilee, one of the cities of refuge: and Hammoth Dor, and Carthan, with their suburbs, three cities.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the cities of the families of Gerson, were thirteen, with their suburbs.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And to the children of Merari, Levites of the inferior degree, by their families were given of the tribe of Zabulon, Jecnam and Cartha,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Damna and Naalol, four cities with their suburbs.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of Ruben beyond the Jordan over against Jericho, Bosor in the wilderness, one of the cities of refuge, Misor and Jaser and Jethson and Mephaath, four cities with their suburbs.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tribe of Gad, Ramoth in Galaad, one of the cities of refuge, and Manaim and Hesebon and Jaser, four cities with their suburbs,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the cities of the children of Merari by their families and kindreds, were twelve.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So all the cities of the Levites within the possession of the children of Israel were forty-eight,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: With their suburbs, each distributed by the families.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord God gave to Israel all the land that he had sworn to give to their fathers: and they possessed it, and dwelt in it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he gave them peace from all nations round about: and none of their enemies durst stand against them, but were brought under their dominion.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Not so much as one word, which he had promised to perform unto them, was made void, but all came to pass.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At the same time Josue called the Rubenites, and the Gadites, and the half tribe of Manasses,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And said to them: You have done all that Moses the servant of the Lord commanded you: you have also obeyed me in all things,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither have you left your brethren this long time, until this present day, keeping the commandment of the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore as the Lord your God hath given your brethren rest and peace, as he promised: return, and go to your dwellings, and to the land of your possession, which Moses the servant of the Lord gave you beyond the Jordan:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Yet so that you observe attentively, and in work fulfil the commandment and the law which Moses the servant of the Lord commanded you: that you love the Lord your God, and walk in all his ways, and keep all his commandments, and cleave to him, and serve him with all your heart, and with all your soul.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Josue blessed them, and sent them away, and they returned to their dwellings.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now to half the tribe of Manasses, Moses had given a possession in Basan: and therefore to the half that remained, Josue gave a lot among the rest of their brethren beyond the Jordan to the west. And when he sent them away to their dwellings and had blessed them,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He said to them: With much substance and riches, you return to your settlements, with silver and gold, brass and iron, and variety of raiment: divide the prey of your enemies with your brethren.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So the children of Ruben, and the children of Gad, and the half tribe of Manasses returned, and parted from the children of Israel in Silo, which is in Chanaan, to go into Galaad the land of their possession, which they had obtained according to the commandment of the Lord by the hand of Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they were come to the banks of the Jordan, in the land of Chanaan, they built an altar immensely great near the Jordan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the children of Israel had heard of it, and certain messengers brought them an account that the children of Ruben, and of Gad, and the half tribe of Manasses had built an altar in the land of Chanaan, upon the banks of the Jordan, over against the children of Israel:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They all assembled in Silo, to go up and fight against them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the mean time they sent to them into the land of Galaad, Phinees the son of Eleazar the priest,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And ten princes with him, one of every tribe.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who came to the children of Ruben, and of Gad, and the half tribe of Manasses, into the land of Galaad, and said to them:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus saith all the people of the Lord: What meaneth this transgression? Why have you forsaken the Lord the God of Israel, building a sacrilegious altar, and revolting from the worship of him?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Is it a small thing to you that you sinned with Beelphegor, and the stain of that crime remaineth in us to this day? and many of the people perished.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you have forsaken the Lord to day, and to morrow his wrath will rage against all Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if you think the land of your possession to be unclean, pass over to the land wherein is the tabernacle of the Lord, and dwell among us: only depart not from the Lord, and from our society, by building an altar beside the altar of the Lord our God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Did not Achan the son of Zare transgress the commandment of the Lord, and his wrath lay upon all the people of Israel? And he was but one man, and would to God he alone had perished in his wickedness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of Ruben, and of Gad, and of the half tribe of Manasses answered the princes of the embassage of Israel:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord the most mighty God, the Lord the most mighty God, he knoweth, and Israel also shall understand: If with the design of transgression we have set up this altar, let him not save us, but punish us immediately:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if we did it with that mind, that we might lay upon it holocausts, and sacrifice, and victims of peace offerings, let him require and judge:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And not rather with this thought and design, that we should say: To morrow your children will say to our children: What have you to do with the Lord the God of Israel?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord hath put the river Jordan for a border between us and you, O ye children of Ruben, and ye children of Gad: and therefore you have no part in the Lord. And by this occasion your children shall turn away our children from the fear of the Lord. We therefore thought it best,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And said: Let us build us an altar, not for holocausts, nor to offer victims,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But for a testimony between us and you, and our posterity and yours, that we may serve the Lord, and that we may have a right to offer both holocausts, and victims and sacrifices of peace offerings: and that your children to morrow may not say to our children: You have no part in the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if they will say so, they shall answer them: Behold the altar of the Lord, which our fathers made, not for holocausts, nor for sacrifice, but for a testimony between us and you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: God keep us from any such wickedness that we should revolt from the Lord, and leave off following his steps, by building an altar to offer holocausts, and sacrifices, and victims, beside the altar of the Lord our God, which is erected before his tabernacle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Phinees the priest, and the princes of the embassage, who were with him, had heard this, they were satisfied: and they admitted most willingly the words of the children of Ruben, and Gad, and of the half tribe of Manasses,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Phinees the priest the son of Eleazar said to them: Now we know that the Lord is with us, because you are not guilty of this revolt, and you have delivered the children of Israel from the hand of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he returned with the princes from the children of Ruben and Gad, out of the land of Galaad, into the land of Chanaan, to the children of Israel, and brought them word again.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the saying pleased all that heard it. And the children of Israel praised God, and they no longer said that they would go up against them, and fight, and destroy the land of their possession.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of Ruben, and the children of Gad called the altar which they had built, Our testimony, that the Lord is God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when a long time was passed, after that the Lord had given peace to Israel, all the nations round about being subdued, and Josue being now old, and far advanced in years:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Josue called for all Israel, and for the elders, and for the princes, and for the judges, and for the masters, and said to them: I am old, and far advanced in years,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you see all that the Lord your God hath done to all the nations round about, how he himself hath fought for you:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And now since he hath divided to you by lot all the land, from the east of the Jordan unto the great sea, and many nations yet remain:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord your God will destroy them, and take them away from before your face, and you shall possess the land as he hath promised you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Only take courage, and be careful to observe all things that are written in the book of the law of Moses: and turn not aside from them neither to the right hand nor to the left:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lest after that you are come in among the Gentiles, who will remain among you, you should swear by the name of their gods, and serve them, and adore them:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But cleave ye unto the Lord your God, as you have done until this day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And then the Lord God will take away before your eyes nations that are great and very strong, and no man shall be able to resist you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: One of you shall chase a thousand men of the enemies: because the Lord your God himself will fight for you, as he hath promised.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This only take care of with all diligence, that you love the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if you will embrace the errors of these nations that dwell among you, and make marriages with them, and join friendships:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Know ye for a certainty that the Lord your God will not destroy them before your face, but they shall be a pit and a snare in your way, and a stumbling-block at your side, and stakes in your eyes, till he take you away and destroy you from off this excellent land, which he hath given you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold this day I am going into the way of all the earth, and you shall know with all your mind that of all the words which the Lord promised to perform for you, not one hath failed,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore as he hath fulfilled in deed, what he promised, and all things prosperous have come: so will he bring upon you all the evils he hath threatened, till he take you away and destroy you from off this excellent land, which he hath given you,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When you shall have transgressed the covenant of the Lord your God, which he hath made with you, and shall have served strange gods, and adored them: then shall the indignation of the Lord rise up quickly and speedily against you, and you shall be taken away from this excellent land, which he hath delivered to you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Josue gathered together all the tribes of Israel in Sichem, and called for the ancients, and the princes and the judges, and the masters: and they stood in the sight of the Lord:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he spoke thus to the people: Thus saith the Lord the God of Israel: Your fathers dwelt of old on the other side of the river, Thare the father of Abraham, and Nachor: and they served strange gods.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I took your father Abraham from the borders of Mesopotamia: and brought him into the land of Chanaan: and I multiplied his seed,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And gave him Isaac: and to him again I gave Jacob and Esau. And I gave to Esau mount Seir for his possession: but Jacob and his children went down into Egypt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I sent Moses and Aaron, and I struck Egypt with many signs and wonders.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I brought you and your fathers out of Egypt, and you came to the sea: and the Egyptians pursued your fathers with chariots and horsemen, as far as the Red Sea.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of Israel cried to the Lord: and he put darkness between you and the Egyptians, and brought the sea upon them, and covered them. Your eyes saw all that I did in Egypt, and you dwelt in the wilderness a long time.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I brought you into the land of the Amorrhite, who dwelt beyond the Jordan. And when they fought against you, I delivered them into your hands, and you possessed their land, and slew them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Balac son of Sephor king of Moab arose and fought against Israel. And he sent and called for Balaam son of Beor, to curse you:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I would not hear him, but on the contrary I blessed you by him, and I delivered you out of his hand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you passed over the Jordan, and you came to Jericho. And the men of that city fought against you, the Amorrhite, and the Pherezite, and the Chanaanite, and the Hethite, and the Gergesite, and the Hevite, and the Jebusite: and I delivered them into your hands.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I sent before you hornets: and I drove them out from their places, the two kings of the Amorrhites, not with thy sword nor with thy bow,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I gave you a land, in which you had not laboured, and cities to dwell in which you built not, vineyards and oliveyards, which you planted not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now therefore fear the Lord, and serve him with a perfect and most sincere heart: and put away the gods which your fathers served in Mesopotamia and in Egypt, and serve the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if it seem evil to you to serve the Lord, you have your choice: choose this day that which pleaseth you, whom you would rather serve, whether the gods which your fathers served in Mesopotamia, or the gods of the Amorrhites, in whose land you dwell: but as for me and my house we will serve the Lord,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the people answered, and said, God forbid we should leave the Lord, and serve strange gods.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord our God he brought us and our fathers out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage: and did very great signs in our sight, and preserved us in all the way by which we journeyed, and among all the people through whom we passed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he hath cast out all the nations, the Amorrhite the inhabitant of the land into which we are come. Therefore we will serve the Lord, for he is our God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Josue said to the people: You will not be able to serve the Lord: for he is a holy God, and mighty and jealous, and will not forgive your wickedness and sins.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If you leave the Lord, and serve strange gods, he will turn, and will afflict you, and will destroy you after all the good he hath done you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the people said to Josue: No, it shall not be so as thou sayest, but we will serve the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Josue said to the people, You are witnesses, that you yourselves have chosen you the Lord to serve him. And they answered: We are witnesses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now therefore, said he, put away strange gods from among you, and incline your hearts to the Lord the God of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the people said to Josue: We will serve the Lord our God, and we will be obedient to his commandments.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Josue therefore on that day made a covenant, and set before the people commandments and judgments in Sichem.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he wrote all these things in the volume of the law of the Lord: and he took a great stone, and set it under the oak that was in the sanctuary of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to all the people: Behold this stone shall be a testimony unto you, that it hath heard all the words of the Lord, which he hath spoken to you: lest perhaps hereafter you will deny it, and lie to the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he sent the people away every one to their own possession,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after these things Josue the son of Nun the servant of the Lord died, being a hundred and ten years old:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they buried him in the border of his possession in Thamnathsare, which is situate in mount Ephraim, on the north side of mount Gaas.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Israel served the Lord all the days of Josue, and of the ancients that lived a long time after Josue, and that had known all the works of the Lord which he had done in Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the bones of Joseph which the children of Israel had taken out of Egypt, they buried in Sichem, in that part of the field which Jacob had bought of the sons of Hemor the father of Sichem, for a hundred young ewes, and it was in the possession of the sons of Joseph.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Eleazar also the son of Aaron died: and they buried him in Gabaath that belongeth to Phinees his son, which was given him in mount Ephraim.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner
